stdout: {"status":"failed","message":"lem:primitive-roy-boundary-subclass-nondegenerate (witness_not_in_alpha1_class) and thm:no-honest-adaptation (lower_bound_not_discharged). The current fast-class witness only changes a core (0,l_n) with l_n<n^(-1), so the operative shell [n^(-1),n^(-beta)] still carries the alpha_0 shell variance and shell-limit behavior. Reproducing membership in P_n(alpha_1) therefore fails at the load-bearing shell conditions, not just at prose wiring. Repairing this honestly would require a new two-point construction that simultaneously (i) makes P_{1n} genuinely satisfy the alpha_1 shell class on the n^(-1)-scale shells, (ii) keeps TV(P_{0n}^n,P_{1n}^n) <= eta, and (iii) preserves an endpoint separation of order rho_n(alpha_0)^(-1). That is genuine new math beyond an in-place core edit; without it, lem:two-alpha-pair-exists and the stated honest-adaptation lower bound are not discharged on the claimed classes."}
